name: Saba (Caribbean Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: the surrounding sea and the sky
the central five-pointed star, representing the island

description: A field divided per saltire (by diagonals) into four triangles: the hoist and fly triangles white, the top triangle red, and the bottom triangle blue. In the centre, a yellow five-pointed star is charged on the white centre.
history: Officially adopted on 6 December 1985 following a public design contest. The design by Marka Hassell was chosen from among the entries; the colours echo those of the Netherlands flag while the central star represents Saba. The flag continued in use after Saba became a special municipality of the Netherlands on 10 October 2010.

features.symbolism: the field divided per saltire into four triangles — white at hoist and fly, red at the chief and blue at the base — echoing the colours of the Netherlands
